name: History-Resolved Loop-Coherent Chern Self-Healing Law
equation: (1) theta_R,e^{+} = theta_R,e + clip(wrap(theta_raw,e - theta_R,e), -pi_a, +pi_a); (2) Theta_p(t)=sum_{e in p} sigma_{p,e} theta_R,e(t), Psi(t)= (1/N_p) sum_{p=1}^{N_p} cos(Theta_p(t)/pi_a); (3) d g_e / dt = alpha_G(S)[1 + kappa_Psi Psi(t)] |J_e| e^{i theta_R,e} - mu_G(S) g_e - lambda_s g_e sin^2(theta_R,e/(2 pi_a)) + chi C_loc(t) g_e
description: An adaptive-conductance law that preserves history-resolved phase with an adaptive ruler (Eq.1), measures topological loop coherence via a plaquette order parameter Psi(t) (Eq.2), and embeds both into a Chern self-healing conductance equation (Eq.3). When kappa_Psi=0 the law reduces to the Chern self-healing form; when chi=0 and C_loc(t)=0 it becomes a loop-aware ARP law driven purely by history-resolved phase.
assumptions: The ARP / adaptive-pi_a framework and units from the existing TopEquations lineage apply.
Plaquette structure and orientation signs sigma_{p,e} are well-defined on the underlying lattice.
Psi(t) is computed over a fixed plaquette set with slowly varying topology (no tearing events during a run).
kappa_Psi >= 0, chi >= 0, and other parameters (mu_G, alpha_G, lambda_s) behave as in the parent laws.
evidence: Builds on leaderboard #1 (History-Resolved Phase with Adaptive Ruler), #2 (Adaptive Chern Self-Healing Conductance Law) and #7 (Topological Coherence Order Parameter).
Reduces to the existing Chern self-healing law when kappa_Psi = 0.
Reduces to a loop-aware ARP law when chi = 0 and C_loc(t) = 0.

Proposed validation: compare locked vs slipping regimes in QWZ and SSH lattices while varying kappa_Psi; show that high Psi(t) correlates with stable edge channels and self-healing after local defects.
